title: LGPL license question
author: Emstar
body:
Hey @cutterbl thanks for sharing this software!

I intend to distribute some free software that uses `SoundTouchJS` and I wanted to know if you agreed with my interpretation of the license. 

I'm using the module via `npm` and just calling it through the API, nothing weird. My TypeScript app will be packaged with Electron.

However, I do switch the `quickSeek` flag to False at runtime. _I didn't change the source code._ 

If I understand the LGPL correctly setting that flag without changing source does not obligate me to republish the source code, or to build my project in such a way that `SoundTouchJS` can be replaced. I would expect the "knobs" exposed to OK use. 

Let me know what you think. Of course, I want to be a good citizen here, but I am also hoping not to do work I don't have to do. :) 

Cheers!

title: How to use SoundTouch with a custom audio source?
author: markboyall
body:
In our existing application, we use native messaging for audio functionality as our application predates WebAudio. We're looking to eliminate our native component and part of this involves audio playback. We've been using SoundTouch on the C# side for some time with no complaints so this seemed like a good choice.

The problem, however, is that our audio is very large; considerably more than will fit in an AudioBuffer. To handle this so far I'm using a custom audio worklet and streaming the audio with postMessage. However it's pretty unclear how to handle changing the playback rate. I thought that the intended outcome would be to resample the input, then supply this as if it's at the original sample rate and use SoundTouch to simply shift the pitch, which avoids buffer starvation as my source node can just stream faster for faster playback rates.

However this system results in choppy audio whether playing faster or slower so I'm feeling like I'm missing some core concept of how the playback rate changing should actually work.

Any thoughts on how the system is meant to work when using a custom audio source?

title: `playbackRate` parameter `maxValue` of 4 prevents pitch correction at higher speeds
author: oronevron
body:
I'm trying to use `@soundtouchjs/audio-worklet` to preserve pitch when playing audio via `AudioBufferSourceNode` at various speeds (up to 8x).

At rates above 4x, the browser clamps `stNode.playbackRate.value` (so the pitch is not preserved) and logs (as a warning):
`AudioWorkletNode("soundtouch-processor").playbackRate.value 8 outside nominal range [0.25, 4]; value will be clamped.`

When I'm locally changing [this line](https://github.com/cutterbl/SoundTouchJS/blob/9408f7232ed9eb0c5d51b52c539115c9ae5ee90f/packages/audio-worklet/src/processor.ts#L67) to `maxValue: 8`, the warning is not shown, and the pitch preservation seems to be working.

Is there a reason `maxValue` set to 4 for this parameter?
Would you be open to increasing the range, or making it configurable?

title: PitchShifter timePlayed is inaccurate
author: jasonmcaffee
body:
PitchShifter's timePlayed is calculated by evaluating the number of frames extracted for a given onaudioprocess event.
Since the frequency at which onaudioprocess is fired can vary, and can potentially only fire once every half second, the timePlayed cannot be accurate.

Without an accurate timePlayed, it's not possible to sync audio and visual elements with timePlayed.

I put together a codepen that demonstrates the issue.  The demo plays audio and then stops it after 1 second, and displays PitchShifter's timePlayed property.  After 1 second, timePlayed is 1.11 - 1.48 on my machine (2018 macbook pro using Chromium 88.0.4324.96).
 
https://codepen.io/jasonmcaffee/pen/WNRrrQm

Thanks!

title: Crackling sound with newest Google Chrome version
author: zoschfrosch
body:
Hi,

great piece of software! I never thougt that online audio processing with javascript is possible, but your library does it with a very good performance. I use your library since several months, until a few days ago without any problems. But with the newest update of Google Chrome from version 89.0.4389.82 to 89.0.4389.90, the sound became distorted and crackling. Same thing with Edge, Firefox works well with no distortions. The problems occur with Windows 10. With Ubuntu 20.04 I cannot reproduce them because Chrome is still on the older version.
Playing the same audio files with the html audio element works fine. I experimented with a greater buffer size, up to 16384. This reduced the distortion, but then the tracks (I have several instrument tracks of the same piece of music) run out of sync.
Do you have any idea what's going on? Are there other users with the same problem?

Kind regards
Thomas

title: Is there a way to perform pitch shifting while keeping multiple audios as synchronized as possible?
author: Gullinkambi-Whip
body:
Thank you for making pitch shifting so easy.

Is there a way to apply pitch-shifting effects to multiple pieces of audio while keeping the audio as synchronized as possible?  

I'm trying to perform pitch shifting on multiple audios at once, while manipulating the gain of each audio.  
The following is a demo.  
[CodeSandBox](https://codesandbox.io/s/dazzling-cloud-kvj23?fontsize=14&hidenavigation=1&theme=dark)

The above demo is basically fine, but on mobile devices or low-spec devices, there may be a delay in the playback of individual audio.  
Also, moving ```input type=range``` will cause the above phenomenon to occur occasionally.

I was taught the following on Stack Overflow how to play multiple audios as synchronously as possible.  
```javascript
audioBufferSourceNode.start(ctx.currentTime)
```
[javascript - Is there any way to change the gain individually in real time when playing a node that is a composite of two AudioBuffers? - Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/65919632/is-there-any-way-to-change-the-gain-individually-in-real-time-when-playing-a-nod/65922959?noredirect=1#comment116588218_65922959)

However, I know that shifterNode does not have a start method.  
So, if you have another solution, I would like to know about it.

title: Audio workers
author: watch-janick
body:
Hello Steve Blades,

First I wanted to thank you for this amazing implementation of this library.

I need to make it work with audio workers for a project, and more specifically on top of `standardized-audio-context`.
Would you be interested in working on the audio workers implementation?
I can sponsor this feature to make it available to the whole community.

We can discuss it further by email at janickdelot@gmail.com

Thanks!
